title: Shri Rudrashtakam
intro: Read Shri Rudrashtakam with Hindi meaning. Chant this sacred hymn to seek the blessings of Lord Shiva.
description:
|| Shri Rudrashtakam ||

1. Namamisha-mishana-nirvana-rupam vibhum vyapakam brahma-veda-swarupam.
Nijam nirgunam nirvikalpam niriham chidakasha-makasha-vasam bhaj-aham.||
2. Nirakaram-omkara-mulam turiyam gira-jnana-gotita-misham girisham.
Karalam mahakala-kalam kripalam gunagara-samsara-param nato-aham.||
3. Tusharadri-sankasha-gauram gambhiram manobhuta-koti-prabhashri-shariram.
Sphuran-mauli-kallolini-charu-ganga lasad-bhala-balendu kanthe bhujanga.||
4. Chalat-kundalam bhru-sunetram vishalam prasannananam nilakantham dayalam.
Mrigadhisha-charmambaram mundamalam priyam shankaram sarvanatham bhajami.||
5. Prachandam prakrishtam pragalbham paresham-akhandam-ajam bhanukoti-prakasham.
Trayah-shula-nirmulanam shulapanim bhaje-aham bhavanipatim bhava-gamyam.||
6. Kalatita-kalyana-kalpanta-kari sada sajjanananda-data purari.
Chidananda-sandoha-mohapahari prasida prasida prabho manmathari.||
7. Na yavad-umanatha-padaravindam bhajanti-ha loke pare va naranam.
Na tavat-sukham shanti santapa-nasham prasida prabho sarvabhutadhivasa.||
8. Na janami yogam japam naiva pujam nato-aham sada sarvada shambhu tubhyam.
Jara-janma-dukhaugha-tatapyamanam prabho pahi apanna-mamisha-shambho.||

Phalashruti:
Rudrashtakamidam proktam viprena hara-toshaye.
Ye pathanti nara bhaktya tesham Shambhuh prasidati.||

|| Iti Shri Ramacharitamanase Uttarakaande Shri Goswami Tulsidasakritam Shri Rudrashtakam Sampurnam ||

faq.a1: Shri Rudrashtakam is a famous and powerful hymn composed by Goswami Tulsidas, found in the 'Uttar Kanda' of the Ramacharitamanas, dedicated to Lord Shiva.
faq.q2: What is the context of this stotra?
faq.a2: According to the Ramacharitamanas, Vibhishana recited this stotra in Lanka to please Lord Shiva and seek his blessings.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of reciting Rudrashtakam?
faq.a3: Reciting this hymn invites the grace of Lord Shiva. It is believed to alleviate mental suffering, fear, and sorrow, and helps the devotee progress toward spiritual liberation.
faq.a4: While it can be recited daily, it is especially auspicious to recite it during Mahashivaratri, on Mondays, during Pradosh Vrat, and throughout the holy month of Shravana.
